scenario: Your English friend, Mark, is planning to visit your hometown next month and has asked for some travel advice.
instruction:
Write an email to Mark. In your email, you should:
• suggest the best tourist attractions to see
• recommend some local dishes he must try
• advise him on what clothes to bring for the current weather
Write at least 120 words.
key_points_required: Advice on clothing based on weather

scenario: You are a tenant in an apartment building and the air conditioner in your living room has stopped working during a heatwave.
instruction:
Write a letter to the building manager, Mr. Smith. In your letter, you should:
• explain the problem with the air conditioner
• describe how this issue is affecting your daily life
• request a specific date and time for the repair
Write at least 120 words.
key_points_required: Impact of the broken AC on the writer

scenario: Your local community is organizing a traditional food festival next weekend and you want to invite your friend, Anna.
instruction:
Write an email to Anna. In your email, you should:
• invite her to the traditional food festival
• provide details about the time and location
• describe some interesting activities that will take place
Write at least 120 words.

scenario: You missed an important group presentation for your English class because you were feeling unwell.
instruction:
Write an email to your teacher, Ms. Green. In your email, you should:
• apologize for your absence from the presentation
• explain your health situation briefly
• ask how you can compensate for the missed grade
Write at least 120 words.

scenario: You recently stayed at the 'Grand View' hotel, but the room was not cleaned properly and the staff were unresponsive to your requests.
instruction:
Write a letter to the Hotel Manager. In your letter, you should:
• give details of your stay and the condition of the room
• complain about the poor attitude of the service staff
• demand a partial refund for the unsatisfactory experience
Write at least 120 words.

scenario: You saw an advertisement for a part-time marketing assistant position at an international firm, but some key details are missing.
instruction:
Write an email to the HR Department. In your email, you should:
• state where you saw the ad and why you are interested
• ask about the specific working hours and salary range
• inquire if the company provides any professional training
Write at least 120 words.
key_points_required: Purpose of writing and interest in the role
Request for hours and pay details

scenario: You bought an expensive laptop from an online store, but it arrived with a cracked screen and the wrong operating system installed.
instruction:
Write a letter to the Customer Service Department. In your letter, you should:
• provide your order number and describe the defects
• explain why you need the correct model urgently for work
• request an immediate replacement or a full refund
Write at least 120 words.
key_points_required: Order information and description of damage

scenario: You are organizing a career orientation week at your university and want to invite a successful alumnus to give a speech.
instruction:
Write an email to Mr. Vinh, a successful former student. In your email, you should:
• invite him to speak at the career orientation week
• explain why his experience would be valuable to current students
• offer flexibility regarding the date and online/offline format
Write at least 120 words.
key_points_required: Justification for his selection as a speaker

scenario: Your company hired a catering service for a high-profile international conference, but the food quality was poor and dietary requirements were ignored.
instruction:
Write a letter to the Manager of 'Elite Catering'. In your letter, you should:
• outline the discrepancies between the agreed contract and actual service
• argue how this failure damaged your company's professional reputation
• insist on a significant financial settlement and a formal apology
Write at least 120 words.
key_points_required: Argument on reputational damage to the firm

scenario: You have been living abroad for a year and are writing to your childhood friend about the cultural identity challenges you have faced.
instruction:
Write a letter to your friend, Nam. In your letter, you should:
• share your experiences of adjusting to a foreign lifestyle
• reflect on how being away has changed your perspective on Vietnamese traditions
• ask for updates on your local neighborhood and mutual friends
Write at least 120 words.
key_points_required: Reflection on cultural identity and traditions
